mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) over one month signals expanding manufacturing activity and GDP growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with INDPRO month-over-month change (Spearman rank correlation < 0.0 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6.0% indicates persistent labor slack, reducing consumer spending power and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6.0, the average 60-day SPX return is non-negative (i.e., zero or positive) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Low policy rates (FEDFUNDS near zero) combined with accelerating consumer prices (CPIAUCSL monthly growth above 0.3%) indicate rising inflation expectations that erode real equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL monthly growth > 0.3%, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: After a quarter of negative GDP growth (GDPC1 decline), a rise in housing starts (HOUST) signals the onset of a recovery in residential investment and consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 qoq < 0 and HOUST is rising, 60-day SPX returns are negative or zero more often than positive over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals a sudden drop in consumer demand, causing downward revisions to forward earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS m/m < -1%, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects robust job creation, supporting incomes and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment (UNRATE) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) create a stagflation scenario that depresses both consumer spending and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both UNRATE > 5.5 and CPIAUCSL m/m > 0.3%, 60-day SPX returns are positive more than 30% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising month-over-month), rising industrial production (INDPRO) is interpreted as increasing capacity pressures that will require further rate hikes, weighing on equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS rises m/m and INDPRO also rises, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.
